decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime and has demonstrated remorse and an attempt to rectify the harm by partially repaying the funds. While embezzlement is a serious breach of trust, a 10-year sentence suggested by the spectator is disproportionately high for a $25,000 theft by a first-time offender. A 6-month period of incarceration combined with restitution requirements and probation is appropriate to serve as a deterrent and punishment while allowing for rehabilitation.
